english: to be ashamed, to feel embarrassed
to pass through, to go past
to pass through, to go past (perfective)
to be located, to be situated
to come from, to issue from
to drop by, to call on
to drop by, to call on (perfective)
to have to, to be forced to
to come to an end, to be spent
to be transferred, to pass on
to be raised, to grow up
to get used to, to fit in
to glance at, to take a look
to believe eagerly, to fall for something
to seize by the throat, to pressure
to talk too much, to blab
to play with fire, to take dangerous risks
to reach a dead end, to be stuck
to strike a chord, to emphasize
to make a choice, to decide
to have in mind, to intend
not to sit idle, to be active
to wear a mask, to pretend
to bear an insult, to put up with
to be very rich, money grows on trees
to stab in the back, to betray
to live carefree, to live merrily
to make a thrust, to attack
to make a pause, to stop
to hold a meeting, to sit (imperfective)
to sit down, to get stuck (perfective)
to long for, to lust after (rare)
to blow around, to surround (imperfective, rare)
to dare, to be bold (perfective, rare)
to dare, to be bold (imperfective, rare)
to appeal, to cry out (perfective, rare)
to hurl down, to topple (imperfective, rare)
to hurl down, to topple (perfective, rare)
to jointly excite, to stir up together (rare)
to carry out, to take out
to drag out, to pull out
to be exhausted, to wear out
to swear, to take an oath
it seems to me, I think
in the face of, in the person of
so to speak, as it were
to be ashamed to look someone in the eye

korean: 아름다운 세상이여 안녕, 영원히 헤어지는 인사
english: farewell to the world, a literary parting
a dark soul, an evil nature
the deeper into the woods, the more firewood
to listen to, to hear out
to be upset, to be dismayed
